macro_events.event_summary: A jet fuel shortage is expected to drive summer airfares sharply higher, squeezing airline margins while benefiting refiners with jet fuel exposure.
primary_drivers: Jet fuel supply tightness raising crack spreads for refiners
Higher fuel input costs compressing airline operating margins
Higher airfares dampening leisure travel demand elasticity

beneficiaries.rationale: Valero is the largest US independent refiner with significant jet fuel/distillate output; tighter jet fuel supply widens distillate crack spreads directly accretive to refining margins.
Phillips 66 refining segment has meaningful jet fuel production on West Coast and Gulf Coast; benefits from elevated jet crack spreads heading into peak summer demand.
Marathon Petroleum is the largest US refiner by capacity with high distillate yield; jet fuel scarcity lifts product realizations across its 13 refineries.
Delek US is a smaller pure-play refiner with high distillate exposure; smaller refiners see outsized margin leverage when jet/diesel cracks widen.
Par Pacific operates Hawaii and West Coast refineries with direct jet fuel sales to airlines; West Coast jet fuel premiums benefit them disproportionately.

losers.rationale: American Airlines runs with the weakest balance sheet among US legacies and limited fuel hedging; jet fuel cost spikes flow almost directly to operating margin compression.
United Airlines has minimal fuel hedging policy; every $0.10/gal jet fuel increase costs ~$400M annually, directly hitting summer earnings guidance.
Delta owns the Trainer refinery which provides partial offset, but net exposure to jet fuel costs remains material; higher fares may also dent demand.
Southwest historically hedged but coverage has rolled off; domestic leisure-heavy network is most exposed to airfare-induced demand destruction at higher prices.
JetBlue runs unhedged and has been operating near breakeven; sustained jet fuel premium could push summer quarter back into losses.
Alaska Airlines is heavily exposed to West Coast jet fuel where supply tightness is most acute; regional fuel basis premiums hurt disproportionately.
Booking Holdings sees demand softness if airfares deter marginal leisure travelers; air-hotel package bookings are correlated with airfare affordability.

headlines: Summer airfares set to surge as jet fuel shortage grips market
